label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is about what you should do when communicating with a pen pal, based on the given passage. The passage mentions a few important points. First, it says that you should share all letters and emails with your parents so they know you're safe. Then it says if something seems... (there's a blank here, maybe it's supposed to be "strange" or "unsafe"), you should stop writing. Also, never meet a pen pal unless a grown-up is with you. Looking at the options: A) Keep all the pen pal's letters and emails in private. That's the opposite of what the passage says. It says share with parents, so A is probably wrong. B) Meet with a classmate. The passage says not to meet unless a grown-up is there. Meeting with a classmate isn't mentioned, and the passage specifically says a grown-up should be with you if you meet. So B is not correct. C) Be sure you are safe at any time. The passage emphasizes safety—sharing with parents, stopping if something seems off, not meeting alone. So C seems correct. D) Share whatever
